title: Emperor's plague storehouse

{{Quote|Once we reach the plague storehouse and get what we need, we'll never have to worry about humans again.|[[Nolaa Tarkona]]|<ref name="TEP" />}}
The '''Emperor's plague storehouse''' was an uncharted [[Asteroid/Legends|asteroid]] in the [[Deep Core/Legends|Deep Core]] which had been one of [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine's]] secret research facilities during the [[Imperial Era/Legends|Imperial era]]. It took six separate jumps into [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]] and twice as many hours to get to the asteroid since there was no direct route available.

==Description==
{{Quote|Though the Emperor had no intention of ever unleashing it, he could not bring himself to destroy such an efficient, useful tool. So he ordered it stored in a hidden weapons depot on a small asteroid station. Then he erased the depot's coordinates from Imperial archives, so that no one knew where the stockpile of his terrible virus lay hidden.|[[Nolaa Tarkona]], on the [[Emperor's Plague]]|<ref name="DoG" />}}
This asteroid depot was a maze of pressurized domes, tunnels and sealed chambers storing various biological weapons capable of affecting a wide range of species including the infamous [[Krytos virus]] and a [[Human/Legends|Human]]-specific virus known as the [[Emperor's Plague]]. Its central hub stored the majority of these viruses and was filled with rows of cylinders and tanks. There was also a series of [[Hangar/Legends|docking bays]] on the rim of the plague storehouse. Several sealed chambers contained the deceased corpses of experimental subjects who were predominantly non-Human or other enemies of the Empire.

==History==
{{Dialogue|Bornan Thul|I wish Fonterrat had never stumbled on this place.|Raynar Thul|I wish the Emperor had never even thought of making this asteroid into a weapons storehouse.|Jaina Solo|Well, what are we gonna do about it?|Zekk|We'll destroy the depot, of course. Isn't that why we're here? Nolaa Tarkona's probably on her way already.|Tenel Ka|First, we must find where the plague itself is stored. Then we can neutralize it.|attr=[[Bornan Thul]], [[Raynar Thul]], [[Jaina Solo Fel|Jaina Solo]], [[Zekk]] and [[Tenel Ka Djo|Tenel Ka]]|src=<ref name="TEP" />}}
This plague storehouse had once been the headquarters of the Imperial [[Scientist/Legends|scientist]] [[Evir Derricote]] who had been responsible for the [[Krytos virus]]—a bioengineered virus which afflicted countless [[Alien/Legends|alien]] [[Species/Legends|species]] following the [[Liberation of Coruscant (Galactic Civil War)|Liberation]] of [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]] in [[7 ABY/Legends|7 ABY]]. Prior to his first death at the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]] in [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]], Palpatine ordered that samples of the human-specific [[Emperor's Plague]] be stored in the hidden weapons depot. He also had the depot's coordinates removed from Imperial archives to hide its location. Only a few high-ranking Imperials were aware of the existence of this station and its depot.

In [[24 ABY/Legends|24 ABY]], an alien [[Scavenger/Legends|scavenger]] named [[Fonterrat]] came across the Emperor's plague storehouse. Though the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] had been defeated, this plague warehouse still remained with its supplies of deadly diseases. He then offered to sell a [[Navigation computer/Legends|navicomputer]] module containing information of the plague storehouse to [[Nolaa Tarkona]] knowing of their [[Anti-Humanism|hatred]] of Humans though he refused to deal directly with her, suggesting that she sent a neutral party to meet him on [[Kuar]].

When negotiating, he also provided her with one sample of the virus which she use to seal his payment. The neutral party turned out to be the a wealthy Human merchant and shipping tycoon named [[Bornan Thul]], the owner of [[Bornaryn Trading]]. In exchange for the navicomputer module that contained the coordinates for the asteroid, Bornan paid with a time-locked case filled with [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|credits]]. Bornan was then supposed to deliver it to Nolaa at a trade conference on [[Shumavar/Legends|Shumavar]] but he found out about what lay within the plague warehouse and went into hiding.

Nolaa also captured the last surviving member of the [[Four unidentified Imperial Royal Guards|four]] [[Emperor's Royal Guard/Legends|Imperial Royal Guards]] who had led the [[Second Imperium]]'s [[Second Imperium Crisis|insurgency]] in [[23 ABY/Legends|23 ABY]] and interrogated him over the location of the weapons depot so that she could lay her hands on its deadly cache of biological weapons. When he refused to cooperate, she had him murdered by using a sample of the Emperor's Plague.

Enraged, Nolaa hired numerous [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunters]] to locate Thul and bring back the navicomputer. Among those bounty hunters was [[Ailyn Vel]], disguised as her father [[Boba Fett/Legends|Boba Fett]]. In response to this, his son [[Raynar Thul]]—who was a [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] trainee at the [[Jedi Praxeum]] on [[Yavin 4/Legends|Yavin 4]]—along with his Jedi companions [[Darth Caedus|Jacen]], [[Jaina Solo Fel|Jaina Solo]], [[Tenel Ka Djo|Tenel Ka]], the [[Wookiee/Legends|Wookiee]] [[Lowbacca]] and his translator droid [[M-TD]] went on his trail. They would later be joined by [[Zekk]]. After encounters with bounty hunters and visits to several worlds including [[Mechis III/Legends|Mechis III]] and [[Ziost/Legends|Ziost]], they caught up with Bornan and learned the reason he had gone into hiding.

They then manage to find the asteroid depot and plant explosives in it. However, a [[Diversity Alliance]] [[Fleet/Legends|fleet]] under Nolaa herself arrived. During the ensuing chaos, Bornan and several of the Alliance members including Nolaa herself were infected with several deadly plagues released from smashed canisters and tanks. Bornan contracted a Human-specific virus and died.

Help for the Jedi arrived in the form of the [[New Republic Defense Fleet/Legends|New Republic Defense Fleet]] and the [[Bornaryn Trading Fleet]] which defeated the Alliance fleet during the [[Battle of the Emperor's Plague Storehouse|ensuing battle]]. After the Jedi evacuated the asteroid depot, the combined New Republic/Bornaryn fleet destroyed the plague warehouse, thus destroying the plagues stored within it. Nolaa Tarkona managed to escape with the Wookiee [[Raabakyysh]] on her [[Transport/Legends|transport]] ''[[Rising Star]]'' though she later died on an uncharted asteroid in the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim]].

==Behind the scenes==
It is unknown how the warehouse came to contain Krytos virus samples, when it was closed off before the virus was actually invented.

==Appearances==
